summary: Remediation attempt 1 added the missing explicit Temporal replay boundary for terminal checkpoint publication. New histories opt in through a replay-stable workflow.patched decision carried in the existing fetch-result activity payload; old histories retain the pre-publication behavior. The verifier's parent checkpoint restoration, durable evidence persistence, eligibility policy, and hermetic journey gaps remain open.

gapStatuses.requirement: Parent-level gate/review failures can publish from the latest authoritative checkpoint when the live workspace is gone.
gapStatuses.evidence: No parent finalization or checkpoint restoration implementation was added in this bounded attempt.
gapStatuses.remaining: Adopt verified child evidence first or restore a digest-validated checkpoint and invoke terminal publication with source=checkpoint_restore.

gapStatuses.requirement: Finish summary, checkpoint binding, artifacts, execution detail, and UI agree on branch name, head SHA, intent, and status.
gapStatuses.evidence: This attempt did not add authoritative checkpoint/git-binding or publication artifact persistence.
gapStatuses.remaining: Persist bounded terminal-publication evidence and derive API output from the durable binding.

gapStatuses.evidence: TERMINAL_CHECKPOINT_PUBLICATION_PATCH_ID gates a new terminalCheckpointPublicationEnabled activity field whose default is false; the activity publishes controlled failures only when enabled. Stable-ID and patched request-shape regression tests were added.
gapStatuses.remaining: Focused tests could not execute because pytest is absent from this runtime; compile and diff checks passed.

gapStatuses.requirement: Hermetic journey coverage proves the end-to-end controlled-failure preservation path.
gapStatuses.evidence: No issue-specific compose-backed journey was added or run in this attempt.
gapStatuses.remaining: Add and run the verifier-specified controlled-failure integration journey.

gapStatuses.requirement: Scan, protected-ref, workspace-authority, credential, lease, read-only, dry-run, and noRemoteWrites protections remain enforced through an explicit eligibility policy.
gapStatuses.evidence: This attempt introduced only the replay/version opt-in and did not expand terminal publication eligibility inputs.
gapStatuses.remaining: Add the typed eligibility policy and stable reason-code matrix at the activity boundary.

targetedLocalChecks.details: The repository audits passed, then Python exited with: No module named pytest.
